name: datev
description: Upload receipts and invoices to DATEV Unternehmen online (Belege online) and look up accessible clients via the DATEV accounting:documents API

requiredApis.name: DATEV accounting:documents API (app registration in the DATEV Developer Portal)

envVars.name: DATEV_CLIENT_ID
envVars.description: OAuth client ID of your registered DATEV app

envVars.name: DATEV_CLIENT_SECRET
envVars.description: OAuth client secret of your registered DATEV app

tools.name: List Clients
tools.description: List the DATEV clients (Mandanten) the connected user may upload documents for, including each client's ID needed by the other tools

tools.name: List Document Types
tools.description: List the document types (e.g. incoming invoices, outgoing invoices, cash receipts) available in Belege online for a specific DATEV client, used to classify uploads

clientId.description: DATEV client ID from List Clients

tools.name: Upload Document
tools.description: Upload a receipt or invoice file to DATEV Unternehmen online (Belege online) for a client so the tax advisor can process it for bookkeeping

clientId.description: DATEV client ID from List Clients

file.description: Base64-encoded content of the document (e.g. a PDF invoice), sent decoded as the binary multipart 'file' part

file_name.description: Filename for the uploaded document, e.g. invoice.pdf

metadata.description: Optional JSON metadata part, e.g. { "document_type": "Rechnungseingang", "note": "..." }. Use List Document Types to find valid document types.

prompts.title: Upload a receipt
prompts.prompt: Upload this receipt to DATEV Unternehmen online for the right client so my tax advisor can book it.

prompts.title: List DATEV clients
prompts.prompt: Show me the DATEV clients I can upload documents for.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: DATEV setup

steps.title: Register in the DATEV Developer Portal
steps.description: Create an account at https://developer.datev.de and register as a developer. DATEV API access is gated through their developer/partner program: browse the API catalog under 'Products', request access to the 'accounting:documents' API, and accept the DATEV interface agreement. DATEV reviews the request before granting credentials.

steps.title: Create an app and get OAuth credentials
steps.description: In the Developer Portal, create an app for the accounting:documents API. Register your redirect URI (your deployment URL + /oauth/callback/datev) and note the client ID and client secret. Set them as DATEV_CLIENT_ID and DATEV_CLIENT_SECRET. The API entitlements (which DATEV APIs your app may call) are bound to the app registration rather than to free-form OAuth scopes.

steps.title: Test against the DATEV sandbox
steps.description: The Developer Portal provides a sandbox environment with demo clients. The sandbox uses separate endpoints: authorization at https://login.datev.de/openidsandbox/authorize, tokens at https://sandbox-api.datev.de/token, and the documents API at https://accounting-documents.api.datev.de/platform-sandbox/v2. Sandbox access is granted with your developer registration, so you can develop without a production DATEV account.

steps.title: Go live
steps.description: Production use requires a completed DATEV go-live/approval process and an end user with DATEV Unternehmen online plus a DATEV login (DATEV SmartLogin or SmartCard). The end user authorizes your app via the OAuth consent flow; tokens are then issued for their DATEV clients.

notes: DATEV's OAuth endpoints follow OpenID Connect; the discovery document is at https://login.datev.de/openid/.well-known/openid-configuration. The token endpoint expects HTTP Basic client authentication and PKCE (S256) is supported.

Most DATEV accounting interfaces (e.g. accounting:extf-files / Buchungsdatenservice) are XML/batch oriented and exchange EXTF format files rather than JSON. This connector deliberately covers only the small JSON/multipart REST surface of the accounting:documents API (client lookup, document types, document upload).
DATEV access tokens are short-lived; refresh tokens are issued and the session is bound to the DATEV user's login. Re-authorization is required when the refresh token expires.
The full accounting:documents API reference is only visible after logging in to the DATEV Developer Portal.
